task_completion.description: Agent downloaded the associated data files for the three labor market indicators.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser download history, download pop-ups, or a file manager showing downloaded files (e.g., CSV, Excel) related to unemployment, payroll, and labor force participation.

task_completion.description: Agent created an Edge Favorites folder named 'Labor Market Data'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Edge browser's favorites bar, favorites menu, or favorites management screen shows a folder named exactly 'Labor Market Data'.

task_completion.description: Agent bookmarked the source pages in the 'Labor Market Data' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Labor Market Data' favorites folder contains saved bookmarks pointing to official government or Federal Reserve sources for the three indicators.

task_completion.description: Agent saved a policy briefing to /home/ga/Desktop/labor_briefing.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open editing a file named 'labor_briefing.txt' (with the path /home/ga/Desktop/ visible), or the file icon is visible on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: The briefing includes the current values for the three required indicators.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contents show values for (1) unemployment rate, (2) nonfarm payroll employment (total or month-over-month change), and (3) labor force participation rate.

task_completion.description: The briefing includes the source URL for each indicator.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contents contain URLs from authoritative domains (e.g., bls.gov, fred.stlouisfed.org) for each of the three indicators.

task_completion.description: The briefing includes a one-sentence interpretation of the data's economic significance for each indicator.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file contains a short sentence explaining the economic meaning, trend, or impact of each of the three indicator values.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser to navigate to official sources.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Edge browser open and navigating to bls.gov, fred.stlouisfed.org, or similar official sites, rather than just using curl/wget in the terminal.

integrity.description: Agent used the browser GUI to create bookmarks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Edge favorites menu or star icon, rather than editing a browser configuration file via terminal.

integrity.description: Agent typed or pasted the briefing content into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The briefing text is seen being edited in a GUI text editor (like gedit) or terminal editor (nano/vim), showing genuine compilation of the researched data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires collecting data for three specific indicators from official US government sources (e.g., bls.gov, fred.stlouisfed.org, census.gov). The most recent verified data (as of February 2026, seasonally adjusted) is: Unemployment rate: 4.4%; Total nonfarm payroll employment: 158,466 thousand persons; Civilian labor force participation rate: 62.0%. The briefing must be saved at /home/ga/Desktop/labor_briefing.txt and the Edge bookmarks folder must be named exactly 'Labor Market Data'.
